summary: Reduce rust-diffforge workspace terminal startup latency after agent readiness checks.

review_items.summary: After authentication, launch screen completion, and agent readiness checks, the workspace enters ready state immediately instead of waiting for a fixed 450ms initialization timer.
The Tauri React shell renders App directly without React StrictMode so development builds do not double-run terminal mount effects.
Authentication, paid-user gating, workspace sync, agent status checks, and terminal command allowlists remain unchanged.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, or data access change is required.

patches.summary: Remove the fixed workspace initialization timer and mark the workspace ready immediately once existing gates clear.
Render App directly instead of wrapping it in React.StrictMode.

compatibility.notes: This changes only local desktop startup timing and development render behavior. Existing auth, billing, workspace API, and terminal spawn contracts are preserved.
